{"step_by_step_thinking": "The query is about using stopko tip irrigation for cleaning and drying the pulp chamber. Among the given options, we need to identify the correct mixture. Stopko tip irrigation is typically used with a sodium hypochlorite (NaOCl) solution for cleaning the root canal, and EDTA for chelating and dissolving calculus. Option A (5.25% NaOCl + 17% EDTA) matches this description. Options B, C, and D involve alcohol or ethanol, which are not typically used for root canal irrigation.", "answer_choice": "A"}
